response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems developer with strong software craftsmanship instincts, but he is a poor match for this role. The position's hard requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is nearly unmet — his C++ exposure is limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis side project. Similarly, the role demands 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems work and demonstrated cloud platform leadership, neither of which appears substantively in his career history. His experience skews heavily toward web application development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js), with only one role (Dunnhumby) touching high-throughput distributed systems. There is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform, managing infrastructure at the required scale, or holding a formal technical leadership position over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: deep TDD practice, CI/CD advocacy, pairing, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
High-throughput distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency design)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, talks, and recurring Refactoring Dojos
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: the role requires 8 years; candidate has roughly 6 months of production C++ (National Instruments) and a thesis project

No cloud compute platform ownership or cloud infrastructure leadership — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes for a side project do not constitute production cloud architecture at the required scale
Career trajectory is full-stack web and application development, not systems/infrastructure engineering, making the seniority gap in the required domain very large
